description: Lock statistical_life_tables to Graunt's 1662 mortality-table work using compiled civic/parish death records rather than merchant double-entry bookkeeping.

checks.reason: The source-checked node remains anchored to Graunt's 1662 Natural and Political Observations on the Bills of Mortality.
Compiled parish and civic mortality records supply the tabulated observations that Graunt converted into survival estimates.
Recordkeeping is the corrected direct data substrate for the mortality-table scope.
Population counting remains a direct statistical-data context for life-table inference.
Double-entry bookkeeping is a merchant accounting practice and is broader than the mortality-record source base used here.
